name: go-for-no
description: Richard Fenton & Andrea Waltz's Go for No! — embracing rejection as progress, setting no-goals to achieve yeses, and building your failure quotient.
useCase: Overcoming fear of rejection, setting rejection-based goals, building failure tolerance, recovering from past failure conditioning.
knownRisks: The fable format may feel too simplistic for some readers
The 'go for no' strategy works best in high-volume sales and outbound prospecting
The approach may not translate to contexts where multiple rejections damage relationships
